sourceMovie.title: The Kissing Booth
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Kissing Booth for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Vince Marcello keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Love, Simon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Love, Simon (2018) clearly understood what makes romance work — the same quality that keeps The Kissing Booth rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Kissing Booth 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Kissing Booth 2 (2020) is the kind of film The Kissing Booth fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The romance overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The DUFF
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The DUFF shares The Kissing Booth's gift for romance — the 2015 film delivers comedy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Perfect Date
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Kissing Booth compelling — the romance, the comedy — shows up in The Perfect Date (2019) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Spectacular Now
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance energy of The Kissing Booth is alive in The Spectacular Now (2013), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The First Time
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The First Time (2012) operates in the same romance as The Kissing Booth, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Kissing Booth 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Kissing Booth 3 (2021) belongs to the same conversation as The Kissing Booth — both anchor their romance in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Tall Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Kissing Booth's romance stayed with you, Tall Girl (2019) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Submarine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Kissing Booth excels in romance, Submarine (2011) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Half of It
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Half of It (2020) is the natural next step after The Kissing Booth: same romance DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Kissing Booth?
faq.answer: Try Love, Simon, The Kissing Booth 2, The DUFF—each shares DNA with The Kissing Booth while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Kissing Booth?
faq.answer: The Kissing Booth (2018) is best described as Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Kissing Booth part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Kissing Booth Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
